rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains several technical and causal claims (calibration head → reduced self-preference bias → improved PPO returns; affine recalibration insufficiency → two-parameter fix); logical coherence of these dependencies is a natural target for any general review.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative results are asserted (0.71→0.54 self-preference rate, 8.4-point PPO gain) and tied directly to design choices; checking whether these numbers substantiate the stated conclusions is centrally relevant.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes four distinct 'first' or 'state-of-the-art' claims; these novelty assertions are the highest-risk content in the passage and any competent reading would scrutinize them.
rationale.R1.7: Marquez and Tan (2021) are cited with a precise characterization of their theoretical result and a direct adoption of their two-parameter head; accuracy of this attribution is load-bearing for the scientific integrity of the calibration argument.
